articles.title: Luke Kennard, Marcus Smart fined by NBA for conduct toward officials
articles.original_title: Luke Kennard, Marcus Smart fined by NBA for conduct toward officials
articles.summary: After a remarkably bad showing from the officials in Sunday’s Game 4, punishments have been handed out… ….to the Lakers. Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard were handed fines of $35,000 and $25,000, respectively, for separate issues involving the officials. Smart’s fine came for “questioning the integrity of game officials,” while Kennard’s was “for directing inappropriate […]

articles.title: Pistons Face Elimination After Turnover‑Heavy Loss to Magic
articles.original_title: Pushed to brink, 60-win Pistons vow 'to be better'
articles.summary: Detroit, the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference, fell to the Orlando Magic in Game 4, committing 20 turnovers. The defeat pushed star guard Cade Cunningham and the Pistons to the brink of a first‑round exit.
key_points: The Pistons committed 20 turnovers in a loss that dropped them to a 3‑1 series deficit.
Orlando's win puts Detroit on the brink of elimination in the opening round.
Cade Cunningham’s performance was highlighted as a focal point for the team's future prospects.

articles.title: Thunder Sweep Suns to Open Playoffs
articles.original_title: OKC again starts playoff run with 1st-round sweep
articles.summary: Oklahoma City continued the pattern from last season’s championship run by sweeping the Phoenix Suns in the first round. The series win gives the Thunder a strong start to their postseason campaign.
key_points: Oklahoma City completed a 4‑0 sweep of Phoenix in the opening round.
The sweep mirrors the Thunder’s dominant first‑round performance from the previous year.
The victory positions the Thunder for a deeper playoff run.

articles.title: Jokic’s Triple‑Double Sparks Nuggets’ Survival in Game 5
articles.original_title: Jokic's triple-double powers Nuggets to a Game 6
articles.summary: Nikola Jokic broke out of a slump with a triple‑double, while Spencer Jones added a crucial spark, as Denver defeated an injury‑plagued Minnesota Timberwolves 125‑113. The win staved off elimination and forced a Game 6.
key_points: Jokic recorded a triple‑double, reviving his playoff performance.
Spencer Jones contributed key scoring and energy for Denver.
The Nuggets’ 125‑113 victory extends the series to a decisive Game 6.

articles.title: Cooper Flagg Wins Rookie of the Year
articles.original_title: Flagg edges Knueppel in tight Rookie of Year vote
articles.summary: Dallas Mavericks forward Cooper Flagg, who broke multiple rookie records, was named the NBA Rookie of the Year on Monday. His breakout season solidified his status as a top young talent.
key_points: Flagg secured the Rookie of the Year award after a record‑setting debut season.
He set new franchise and league rookie milestones during the year.
The honor highlights Flagg’s rapid development and impact for the Mavericks.

articles.title: Federal Prosecutors Seek New Charges Against Terry Rozier
articles.original_title: Feds: Plan to file new charges against Rozier
articles.summary: U.S. prosecutors announced plans to file superseding charges of sports bribery and honest‑services wire fraud against NBA player Terry Rozier. The move follows an ongoing investigation into alleged betting‑related misconduct.
key_points: Rozier faces additional federal charges of sports bribery and honest‑services wire fraud.
The superseding indictment reflects the continuation of a broader investigation.
An assistant U.S. attorney confirmed the intent to file the new charges.

articles.title: Riley Stays the Course, No Retirement Plans After Heat Miss Playoffs
articles.original_title: Riley not retiring, sticking with 'win big' approach
articles.summary: Pat Riley confirmed he is not retiring and will maintain his "win big" philosophy despite Miami missing the postseason for the first time since the 2018‑19 season. The Heat organization expects Riley to continue shaping the roster and culture moving forward.
key_points: Riley explicitly ruled out retirement following the Heat's playoff miss.
He reaffirmed his aggressive "win big" approach to team building.
The statement comes as Miami evaluates roster changes after ending a long playoff streak.

articles.title: Lakers Guards Smart and Kennard Fined for On‑Court Verbal Spat with Officials
articles.original_title: Smart, Kennard fined for verbal spat with officials
articles.summary: Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard were each fined by the NBA after a heated exchange with referees following the Lakers' 115‑96 Game 4 loss to the Houston Rockets. The league released the disciplinary action on Monday.
key_points: Both players received monetary fines for confronting officials.
The incident occurred immediately after the Lakers' Game 4 defeat to Houston.
NBA officials emphasized the importance of respecting referees during games.

articles.title: NBA Offseason Guides for All 15 Eliminated Teams
articles.original_title: 🗺️ NBA offseason guides for all eliminated teams
articles.summary: ESPN outlines the offseason outlook for each of the 15 NBA franchises that missed the playoffs, including the Suns, Warriors, Heat, and Bucks. The guides cover roster options, draft positioning, and potential strategic moves for the upcoming summer.
key_points: Comprehensive breakdown of offseason scenarios for every non‑playoff team.
Analysis includes likely free‑agent targets, trade possibilities, and draft outlooks.
Provides context for how each franchise can improve heading into the next season.

articles.title: Lakers Aim to Close First‑Round Series Against Rockets
articles.original_title: Lakers look to secure series victory over the Rockets
articles.summary: With a 3‑1 series lead, the Los Angeles Lakers travel to Houston seeking to clinch the Western Conference first‑round matchup. Betting lines list the Lakers as -2.5 favorites and set the over/under at 208.5 points.
key_points: Lakers hold a 3‑1 advantage over the Rockets in the best‑of‑seven series.
Vegas odds favor Los Angeles by 2.5 points with a 208.5 total.
A win would give the Lakers their fourth straight postseason series victory.

articles.title: Desmond Bane Leads Magic to 3‑1 Lead Over Pistons
articles.original_title: Desmond Bane scores 22, No. 8 seed Magic beat No. 1 seed Pistons for a 3-1 series lead
articles.summary: Desmond Bane scored 22 points as the Orlando Magic defeated the Detroit Pistons 94‑88, taking a 3‑1 series lead and moving within one win of eliminating the No. 1 seed. It would be the Magic's first playoff series win in 16 years.
key_points: Magic up 3‑1 in the series, one win away from ousting the top‑seeded Pistons.
Bane contributed 22 points; Franz Wagner added 19 in three quarters.
Victory positions Orlando for its first postseason series triumph since 2008.
